---
title: "ITP-Shopify Store - Assignment 4 - Collections and Navigation"
author: "Grace Sen"
date: today
format: 
  html: 
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
    number-sections: true
    code-link: true
    embed-resources: true
editor: visual
execute: 
  freeze: auto
---

# **Screenshots**

## Collections in the Shopify admin

![](images/collections.png)

## Collection pages on the storefront

![***Gift Ideas***](images/gift.png)

![***Pantry Favorites***](images/pantry.png)

![***Fresh Produce***](images/produce.png)

## Main navigation menu on the storefront

![](images/navigation menu.png)

# **Merchandising logic explanation**

I organized the products into collections based on how customers naturally shop for farm products. Fresh fruits and vegetables were grouped into ***Fresh Produce***, pantry items such as honey and jams were placed in ***Pantry Favorites***, and customizable baskets and specialty items were grouped under ***Gift Ideas***. This organization keeps similar products together and makes the catalog easier to understand.

# **Customer experience explanation**

A clear navigation menu helps customers quickly find the products they are looking for without searching through the entire catalog. Organizing products into collections reduces browsing time and creates a smoother shopping experience. Customers can easily move between different product categories while exploring the store.

# **CPP Farm Store application reflection**

This assignment showed me how important product organization is for an online store. I think CPP Farm Store could improve its website by creating collections such as Fresh Produce, Pantry Favorites, Gift Baskets, and Seasonal Specials. A simple navigation structure would make it easier for customers to browse products, discover seasonal items, and shop online more efficiently.

# Appendix

GitHub Repo:

GitHub Pages:
